title: Quantum resource redistribution drives spectral splits in dense neutrino gases
abstract: Whether a quantum many-body system can be efficiently simulated hinges not only on its size but also on how quantum resources are organized within it. We characterize the quantum resource landscape of collective neutrino oscillations using entanglement entropy, non-local magic, and matrix product state bond dimension. Using tensor network simulations of neutrinos in the two-flavor sector, we demonstrate that spectral splits--sharp energy-dependent flavor swaps--emerge precisely where entanglement entropy is maximized and non-local magic is minimized locally. This anticorrelation reveals that spectral splits arise not from generic resource growth but from a structured redistribution among flavor modes. The resource dynamics trace constrained arcs in the entanglement-magic phase space, bounded by entanglement spectrum normalization. These findings establish a direct, quantitative link between quantum resources governing computational complexity and astrophysical observables, informing the design of tensor network and quantum circuit simulations of dense neutrino environments.
summary: This paper demonstrates that the emergence of spectral splits in dense neutrino gases is driven by a specific redistribution of entanglement and non-local magic. By mapping these dynamics in a quantum resource phase space, the authors show that splits occur at the intersection of maximal entanglement and minimal non-local complexity. These findings provide a roadmap for optimizing both classical tensor network and future quantum circuit simulations of neutrino environments.
main_problem: The study investigates how the redistribution of quantum resources, specifically entanglement and non-local magic, drives the emergence of spectral splits in dense neutrino gases.
main_result: Spectral splits arise from a structured redistribution of quantum resources where bipartite entanglement is maximized and non-local magic is locally minimized, rather than from generic complexity growth.
method: The authors use tensor network simulations, specifically Matrix Product States (MPS) with the Time-Dependent Variational Principle (TDVP) and Global Subspace Expansion (GSE).
model_or_system: A dense, two-flavor N-neutrino system modeled by an all-to-all spin Hamiltonian in the mass basis, representing neutrino-neutrino forward scattering in core-collapse supernovae.
key_observables: Entanglement entropy, non-local magic (non-stabilizerness), MPS bond dimension, spectral splits (flavor swaps), and antiflatness.
important_parameters: Neutrino-neutrino interaction strength (mu), vacuum oscillation frequency (omega), system size (N=8, 12, 14), and initial flavor configurations.
main_assumptions: The study focuses on the two-flavor sector and utilizes bond dimension truncation as a proxy for computational complexity.
figures_summary: Figures show the dynamics of entanglement and magic, trajectories in the entanglement-magic phase space, the relationship between resource metrics and bond dimension, and asymptotic values of flavor survival probability across different system sizes.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of resource organization, describes the neutrino Hamiltonian and tensor network methods, presents results on resource redistribution and phase space dynamics, and concludes with implications for classical and quantum simulations.
why_interesting: It provides a quantitative link between abstract quantum information measures (like magic and entanglement) and observable astrophysical phenomena, offering insights into how complexity landscapes dictate the efficiency of many-body simulations.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Dynamics of entanglement entropy(top panels) and non-local magic(bottom panels) for neutrinos with frequencies ωi for initial states |Ψ(t0)⟩= |νe⟩⊗12(right panels) and |νµ⟩⊗6 ⊗|νe⟩⊗6 (left panels).

FIG. 2. Asymptotic values of normalized entanglement entropy S, non-local magic M (NL) 2 , antiflatness 4F, and Pν for a 12 neutrinos system with initial configuration |Ψ(t0)⟩= |νµ⟩⊗3 ⊗|νe⟩⊗9 (left) and |Ψ(t0)⟩= |νµ⟩⊗6 ⊗|νe⟩⊗6 (right).

FIG. 3. Dynamics of non-local magic and entanglement entropy of neutrinos with frequency modes ω1(ω12) and ω3(ω10) in phase space for the initial configuration |Ψ(t0)⟩= |νµ⟩⊗6 ⊗|νe⟩⊗6. The solid line comes from the constraint on the entanglement spectra that λ0 +λ1 = 1. The dashed line is at a 45◦angle to emphasize the position of arc in phase space. The final position of the entropy and magic are denoted by the blue dots on the arc.

FIG. 4. Dependence of non-local magic and entanglement entropy on bond dimension in the asymptotic limit for neutrinos with frequency modes ω1 through ω6 for the initial configuration |Ψ(t0)⟩= |νµ⟩⊗6 ⊗|νe⟩⊗6. Opacity is proportional to bond dimension, corresponding to max d = 64, 48, and 36 respectively.
